item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I love the concept of this SCP, it's genius but such a strange sounding SCP!

item.title: Re: Broken link
item.content:encoded: Thanks Nick!

item.title: Re: Broken link
item.content:encoded: Thank you, I've corrected this!

item.title: Broken link
item.content:encoded: Following the link for supplemental document 3166-1 no longer seems to work — garfield.com belongs to Nick now, and the comic archive is over at gocomics.com. The new link should be https://www.gocomics.com/garfield/1989/10/23, I believe.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's silly and seems more like a parody like an actual SCP. Like the 420-J "What if an actual thing/fictional character/etc was actually a SCP?" This can be done well (like the Sasquatch one), but this one falls flat for me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: How come?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This should be a -J

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
+ show block
– hide block

I͚̮̺͆'̻̐ͦ̈́ͬ͝m͈͓͕̰ͨͮ̀͋̔ͅ ̶͚s̛͈̫̹͕̲̰o̵̜̭̣͋̃ͅr̻̥̱͇̬̙̄̓̑̅̇̀ͧr͆y͇͙̝͖̮̯͈͛͐́̉ͤ̄̔͝ ͎̬̫̰̥̼͈̓J̝͓̦̲̭̺ͤ́ͪ͗̓̚ọ͖͙̮̹͍̤̋ͫ́n͛ͭ̾̔.̔́̎͟.͇̮͍͈̥͈.̹̘̳̳̝͓ͩ͘

No uncollapsed Zalgo text in the forums, please. ~Zyn

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: what why no stop bruh why no this is fucked up oh nay why what

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The story arc, referred to in this article as Supplementary Document 3166-1, was a series of strips released before Halloween of 1989 where Garfield found himself in the abandoned remains of his house, with barely any ounce of patented Garfield humor to be seen.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: What is it?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm sorry Jon.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm sorry Jon.

item.title: +1 for Lasagna
item.content:encoded:
This is…different…
I read Garfield all the time as a kid…and the particular comic strips you showed I had forgotten about. (To be fair, they had been made well before I was born.)

…and now I'm a little disturbed…

This also begs the question: what happens when Jim Davis dies…?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Well, this is now my headcanon for this SCP. Also, I want the last two hours of my life back.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Also pretty on-point for this article: r/imsorryjon

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The real question—which mad man is going to make a Square Root of Minus Garfield about this sucker? Being quite the connoisseur of Garfield mashups and parodies (I frequently contribute to the aforementioned SRoMG), I'd like to see someone try that.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I really like that this "explains" Garfield's undying popularity and the October strips, I think explaining real world phenomenon with a SCP is one of the more creative and challenging things an author can do here. But when it comes to the actual SCP itself, I am not all that into it. Sure, Jim-meat being sucked up by mosquitoes sort of added to it, but a Garfield suit filled with lasagna isn't really creatively enticing to me. Maybe it's too obvious? I'm not really sure, but something felt empty to me, like I had already heard the punchline by the time I read "a crudely-made costume of the character Garfield".

Overall it's enjoyable enough, but feels more like a sub-par -J entry.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
this is so disturbing and weird to think about the implications. +1
wish there was more though. it feels like it ends with too many loose ends, unless im missing something.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thanks! I hate it!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: My first +1 on the site, great article!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Garfield is love, Garfield is life.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: "Dr.Bright is longer allowed to show Garfilef videos to SCP-3166" Now that's an idea for an Article.

Oh no I must warn Fatal Farm.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Iz E a tS JoHnn,,, I t i s W Ha T I dO

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I feel like this needs a photo of someone in a creepy Garfield costume to really add some impact to it. I would suggest a specific image (there's a good one of a guy in costume standing in front of a Chinese restaurant at night that looks almost unsettling) but honestly, a search of "China Garfield Costume" gives so many good options that you're spoiled for choice.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thanks, I will!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Seriously, you should really think about just posting this as a tale.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The imagery this conjures is so wonderfully unsettling.

+ Show
- Hide

You walk down the street, on your way home after a long night. It's not yet fully raining, but there's still a slight drizzle about. You had the foresight to wear a waterproof jacket, but while it keeps you dry it does nothing against the chill of the quiet night air.

The office wasn't a pleasant place today. Apparently the strip isn't doing so well, and the numbers are going down. That's bad news for you, as if layoffs become necessary you'll be the the first one on the chopping block. Being a new hire is rough. You can't say that the fading popularity surprises you, it's been the same stale strip for years now. But apparently some investors were really upset. Some suits were prowling around today, asking questions and taking names.

You stop for a minute, pulling out your phone to confirm a sneaking suspicion. Yup, you're headed the wrong way, you shouldn't be anywhere near the park. It's easy to get lost in the rain. You sigh and turn around, resigning yourself to another miserable 10 minutes of slogging through the rain.

There's a figure a ways ahead of you, standing still under a streetlight.

You stop again, mildly unsettled by this. You call out to them, asking if they are okay, but no response comes. Instead the figure begins to plod forwards, taking a step into the light.

The baggy suit slumps and sags with each step, the matted orange fur too dirty to be slicked down by the rain. The lazy-eyed face is at an angle, crudely propped up upon the head, swaying as it advances. The tail drags in the puddles, only adding on to the stains already coating it and the rest of the body.

It hefts the old baseball bat it carries, picking up speed.

You stumble back, beginning to run away as your fear catches in your throat, emerging as a strangled cry. You alternate between turning around and glancing back over your shoulder, desperate to escape but unwilling to let the thing out of your sight. It thumps the bat against the fence as it runs, a sickening thwack sounding with each blow.

Too focused on the beast, you don't avoid the puddle and your foot slides out from under you, sending you crashing to the ground. As the world whirls around you, seeing nothing but darkness and rain, a low agonized meow sounds from above you, immediately followed by a crushing blow to the hip. You scream out in pain, but as you gasp for breath a horrible, wet tearing noise fills the air accompanied by another guttural screech. Then, a dripping, slimy mass is slammed into your face, and your monstrous assailant begins forcing fistfuls more down your throat.

Man, you hate Mondays.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Exactly, an explanation involving the SCP Foundation for why Garfield keeps continuing despite how much it sucks, is one of the best ideas I've ever heard of in my life.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
ridiculous concept: check
body horror: check
pasta in a fursuit: check

yup, this ticks all the boxes for a perfect article. well done.

in all seriousness, though, the idea of the Foundation having to keep Garfield relevant to prevent a lasagna monster from appearing sounds like a -J; however, i feel like this works perfectly well as a mainlist.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: How about Garfield without Garfield, but this entire SCP? No comic strips, just a pile of mosquito-spit lasagna, harassing and feeding itself to people that make lasagna not exist.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You had me at the moment you mentioned Garfield. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: He eats, Jon. It's what he does.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This skip and that comic strip hit a high measurement on my WTF? scale. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I have many questions about that set of comics.

I'm totally and completely fine with this SCP, but those comics are freaky.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Individuals involved in parodies of the Garfield franchise.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
ahahhahahah oh my god what the fuck is this

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I genuinely hate this article, but I hate the upvote that I gave it even more…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The cannibalism really seals the deal.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Okay, what the hell is up with that Garfield storyline? That was weird.

Great use of a real world hook, +1.

Nitpick:

However, analysis of SCP-3166's composition in the field has shown that its interior mass is composed entirely out of pasta: specifically, lasagna.

I think this phrase would be more in style of SCP if it was:

However, analysis of SCP-3166's composition in the field has shown that its interior mass is composed out of lasagna.

Or "of pasta and cooked meat", I don't know.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
So it's a…

LASAGNACAT?
[Infinirimshot]

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The Garfield media franchise is to remain active and successful for as long as feasibly possible.

Well that was a fast +1.

This seems like a Keter threat. Its containment procedures involve significant financial intervention, along with around the clock surveillance of a bunch of plausible targets, and the cover-up for any public manifestations would be a huge headache (imagine GarGhost manifesting on a city street or subway). And what on earth are they going to do once Jim Davis dies?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is extremely stupid and borders on -J material, but I can't help but find it brilliant. The use of the imagination arc blew my mind, and the special containment procedures are a good explanation of why that stale-ass strip is still around.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: No shame in liking Britney Spears.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Garfield is repetitive and bland, not to mention that they have such a big ego and-

Fuck, I take it back +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Upon reaching its target, SCP-3166 will attempt to inflict bodily harm upon them through a mixture of blunt force using nearby objects and force-feeding of lasagna, obtained through self-disembowelment.

[…] genetic analysis of the meat present within the lasagna has shown it to be genetically identical to Garfield creator Jim Davis.

+1ed so hard it hurt. Great job.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: "The Garfield media franchise is to remain active and successful for as long as feasibly possible" is legitimately one of the best hooks I have ever read on this site, because you see that and you know, you know that outside intervention from a shady para-governmental organization is the only reason that limpid fossil continues to persist and excel at what it does. Fuck.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I always knew that bastard was up to something.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I thought the same thing. It did sort of go in a different direction though.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This would be equally good as a -J. +1 anyway because I was such a Garfield fan when I was little.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: No shame in enjoying Garfield.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I, much to my continuing shame1, was a massive Garfield fan as a child, and that arc also freaked me out.

Footnotes
1. For other things I'll never live down, the first two CDs I owned were Britney Spears's Oops I Did it Again and Eiffel 65's Europop.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The hits just keep coming. We had collections of old Garfield comics in our house growing up, and that arc always freaked me out as a kid.

The mainlist feels a little more complete now that we can say we have “the Garfield one.”

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: https://www.youtube.com/user/lasagnacat for anyone unaware. Warnings for assorted generally unpleasant imagery.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: the aesthetic for this SCP is probably Lasagna Cat.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Copyright nerd answer (note: not a Staff member): because the article only contains a link to the comic strip and does not reproduce its contents in any way, it shouldn't be considered copyright infringement. Precedent on this: a decade or so ago there was a legal firm (name escapes me) that sued web forums for linking to news articles; all of their cases were thrown out of court and the firm shut down.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is almost as dark as the "Garfield without Garfield" strips. Well done!

I particularly liked that Jim Davis has been one of the targets.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Tanhony is love, Tanhony is life. +1
